footer.seasonPre: Open call Aug 1–31 · Season 01 opens Sep 1, 2026

auth.joinSubtitle: To collect and save favorites, or to apply as an artist — upload your works now or whenever you like.
auth.bioPh: Two or three sentences about your work and what drives you to make it.
auth.worksNote: After confirming your email you enter your account and upload your works (up to 3: image, title and starting price). You can do it right away or later — your account stays active either way.

picker.collectDesc: I want to explore hand-picked emerging artists and collect before the world discovers them.
picker.artistDesc: I want MANCHA to review my work and present it to collectors next season.

collectors.num1Note: We choose artists before they're obvious. When you reach MANCHA, you arrive first.
collectors.num2Note: Only three pieces per artist. No endless catalogue. Each one's best, with room to be seen properly.
collectors.num3Note: It's not a permanent catalogue. When the season ends, what didn't sell is gone. For good.
collectors.quote: "We don't sell art. We present artists someone believed in first."
collectors.manifestoP1: The difference between a collector and someone buying decorative art is judgement. At MANCHA, the judgement is already made. We review by hand, choose few, and protect the collector's attention.
collectors.manifestoP2: No algorithms, no popularity, no follower count. The work speaks first. What you find here has already passed a filter most artists do not.
collectors.step1Body: A small, curated catalogue. Few artists, few pieces. Just enough for something to stop you.
collectors.step2Body: You choose a piece and place your offer above the minimum bid. No extra fees on the buyer's side.
collectors.step3Body: If someone outbids you, you can bid again whenever you like. The season lasts three months.
collectors.step4Body: If your bid closes first, we coordinate payment and shipping directly with you by email.
collectors.piecesAvailable: {count, plural, one {# piece available} other {# pieces available}}
collectors.avisoSub: The gallery opens September 1. Leave your email and we'll let you know the moment the catalogue is ready — before anyone else.

artists.num1Note: Not an open catalogue. A hand-made selection, season by season.
artists.num2Note: Their best. No filler. No catalogue leftovers.
artists.num3Note: Of every sale. No cost to apply or to exhibit. We only charge if there's a sale.
artists.quote: "Emerging art doesn't die from lack of talent. It dies in the noise."
artists.manifestoP1: At fairs where hundreds of works compete for a three-second glance. In profiles lost to the scroll. In portfolios no one opens.
artists.manifestoP2: MANCHA is the institutional answer to that problem. Few artists per season — the ones we choose, not the ones who managed to sign up. Each shows exactly three pieces. That restriction forces you to show your best.
artists.stmt1: We don't look at your followers.
artists.step1Body: No gallery CV, no perfectly polished portfolio. Just what you've made, in your own words.
artists.step2Body: A person, not an algorithm. If your work makes it in, we write to you directly with next steps.
artists.step3Body: Your own space for the whole season. Collectors bid live, with no middlemen.
artists.step4Body: Of every sale, straight to you. MANCHA handles the rest: shipping, communication, logistics.
artists.stepsCtaNotify: Notify me when the open call starts →
artists.avisoSubPre: Leave your email and we'll let you know the exact moment the open call opens.
artists.avisoSubConv: The open call closes August 31. Season 01 opens September 1.
artists.avisoSubActive: Once you're in MANCHA, you stay in the registry forever.

about.num1Note: Not an open catalogue. A hand-made selection, season by season.
about.num2Note: Their best. No filler. No catalogue leftovers.
about.num3Note: When it closes, it's gone. What you see today may not be available again.
about.manifBig1: Emerging art dies in the noise.
about.manifBody1: At fairs where hundreds of works compete for a three-second glance. In profiles lost to the scroll. In portfolios no one opens. The problem isn't a lack of talent — it's a lack of judgement.
about.manifBig2: MANCHA is the institutional answer to that problem.
about.manifBody2: A season has few artists — the ones we choose, not the ones who managed to sign up. Each shows exactly three pieces. Not one more. That restriction isn't a whim: it forces you to show your best, gives each work room to truly be seen, and protects the collector's attention. The judgement is the product.
about.rule2: The restriction isn't a flaw. It's the point.
about.rule3: The creator takes the larger share. Always.
about.rule4: There is no open catalogue. There is selection.
about.rule5: What you see today may not be here tomorrow.
about.filterTitle1: We don't look at your followers.
about.filterP1: Every application is reviewed by a person, not an algorithm. We don't care if you have a thousand followers or none, whether you trained at an academy or taught yourself. We care about one thing: that the work has something the rest hasn't seen yet.
about.filterP2: Most applications don't make it. Not as punishment — as standard. Every artist we accept carries the MANCHA name with them.
about.pujaConvP1: Applying to MANCHA is free. We review every application personally — no automatic filters, no follower metrics. If your work makes it in, we tell you directly and guide you through uploading your pieces.
about.pujaConvP2: When Season 01 opens on September 1, collectors will be able to explore, bid and take your work home. You keep the larger share of every sale. Always.
about.pujaDefP1: Each piece has a minimum bid and is auctioned throughout the season. When it closes, the highest bidder takes the work — and pays exactly that, with no extra charges on the buyer's side.
about.pujaDefP2: No middlemen, no hidden fees, no fine print. Just the work, the price you set, and the artist who made it.
about.etymQuote: "A mancha is the first gesture on the blank canvas."
about.etymBody: Before control, before technique — pure intention. It's also what remains, what doesn't fade, what marks. The name says both at once: the beginning of something and the trace it leaves. Artists who pass through MANCHA stay in the registry. That doesn't change.

criterioPage.sub: It's not an algorithm. It's not a checklist. It's a standard — and it's written so you know exactly what to aim for.
criterioPage.introText: Y Combinator publishes what it looks for in a startup. We publish what we look for in a work. The standard stops being implicit — the artist knows what to aim for, the collector knows what to trust.
criterioPage.p1Body: A technically flawless work with nothing to say doesn't make it. An imperfect work you can't get out of your head does. What we're after is something we haven't seen yet — not something well executed on top of what already exists.
criterioPage.p2Title: The name doesn't count. The work does.
criterioPage.p2Body: We don't look at your followers. We don't care whether you come from an academy or taught yourself, whether you've shown in galleries or never have. We review the work. That's all. Every application is read by a person, not an algorithm.
criterioPage.p3Body: We're not a permanent catalogue. Three months, a small group, and when it closes it's gone. That restriction isn't a whim — it's what gives being inside its weight. An artist who enters MANCHA doesn't enter a listing. They enter an event.
criterioPage.p4Title: Most don't get in. That's not a punishment.
criterioPage.p4Body: It's the reason getting in matters. The standard is what gives the mark its value. A space that accepts everyone distinguishes no one. We reject not to punish — but so that when someone gets in, the gesture carries real weight.
criterioPage.closeLine: If you believe your work has something the rest hasn't seen yet,
criterioPage.closeAccent: that's exactly what we need to read.

selected.sub: Every artist who entered MANCHA stays here forever. Season by season — the lineage builds from day one.
selected.metaArtists: {count, plural, one {# artist selected} other {# artists selected}}
selected.emptyText: The artists for this season are being selected.
selected.archiveSub: They stay here forever. The record of who came before.

catalog.subCount: {count, plural, one {# piece at auction} other {# pieces at auction}}
catalog.subTail: — each one hand-picked. What you see today may not be here tomorrow.
catalog.subEmpty: The catalogue is revealed once the artists are confirmed. Selection is underway.
catalog.emptyTitle: The catalogue is revealed once the artists are confirmed.
catalog.emptyNote: Selection is underway. When the artists upload their works, you'll find them here with every detail to bid.
catalog.countFiltered: {shown} of {total, plural, one {# piece} other {# pieces}}

piece.followCount: {count, plural, one {# person is following this work} other {# people are following this work}}
piece.bidsCount: {count, plural, one {# bid placed} other {# bids placed}}
piece.discovery: A unique piece by this artist this season. When it closes, it doesn't return.
piece.soldText1: This piece has found its collector. It's unique — there's no other like it.
piece.soldText2: Fell for it anyway? Write to us. If there's real interest, we'll check with the artist whether there's something more to show or a way to reach an agreement.
piece.soldWaMsg: Hi, I saw that "{title}" by {artist} on MANCHA has already sold, but I'm really interested. Is there any possibility?
piece.soldWaBtn: Write to us about the piece
piece.bidTrust: By bidding you commit to pay if you're the highest offer when the season closes. We contact you by email to coordinate — no extra costs.
piece.waMsg: Hi, I'm interested in the piece "{title}" by {artist} on MANCHA. Is it available?
piece.waBtn: Want it now? Message us on WhatsApp
piece.waitlistSub: Leave your email and we'll let you know if there's news about this piece.
